content: I will update the migration file [20260601020000_website_pooja_products.sql](file:///Users/sahilpatel/Desktop/New_Mantra_puja.nosync/APP/supabase/migrations/20260601020000_website_pooja_products.sql) to add the seed entry for `homepage_category_images`. This initializes the settings key in the database.
thinking:
**Confirming Typecheck Success**

I've confirmed the typecheck's success without errors! Now, I'm shifting focus to crafting the SQL migration file for Supabase, as requested. The task involves generating a new migration file within the `supabase/migrations/` directory that defines the seed record for `homepage_category_images`.


**Deciding Migration Strategy**

I'm now deciding between either modifying the existing `20260601020000_website_pooja_products.sql` migration or creating a new one named `20260601030000_homepage_category_images.sql`. I've examined lines 105-118 of the existing migration, which seem like a good reference. Since the changes relate to website settings, either approach seems valid, so I will now choose the preferred strategy.

args.Description: "Add homepage_category_images seed configuration key to website_settings table inside the SQL migration file."
args.Instruction: "Add seed configuration for homepage_category_images settings key."
args.ReplacementContent:
"-- Seed initial settings values if they do not exist
INSERT INTO public.website_settings (key, value)
VALUES 
(
  'store_categories',
  '["Rudraksha","Bracelet","Murti","Yantras","Anklet","Frames","Rashi","Karungali","Jadi","Pyrite","Kavach","Siddh Range","Gemstones","Pyramid","Necklaces/Mala","Tower & Tumbles","Crystal Dome Trees","Women Bracelets","Evil Eye","Gifting"]'::jsonb
),
(
  'view_all_settings',
  '{"banner_image":"https://pub-3027d8d3defe4496978413d3c630aa44.r2.dev/banner/ChatGPT Image May 26, 2026, 12_19_59 PM.png","title":"pujas","heading":"ALL","subheading":"Pure Vedic Seva + Divine Blessings"}'::jsonb
),
(
  'homepage_category_images',
  '{}'::jsonb
)
ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING;
"
